summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on two zone change cases. Case ZC-2021-002 involved Jeffrey Kelly's request to rezone 6.436 acres from R3 to M2 for a truck repair garage on Goshen Road, which the commission unanimously denied after significant neighbor opposition and a county planning commission recommendation of denial. Case ZC-2021-003 involved TPT Holdings' request to rezone 17.736 acres at 6600 Patricia Boulevard from R1/PBDD to M1 for a platform tennis court assembly facility, which generated extensive neighbor opposition regarding traffic, noise, safety, and property values. The commission discussed potentially using a Planned Development Manufacturing (PDM) designation instead of straight M1 zoning for the second case.
decisions: Case ZC-2021-002 (Kelly, 6823 Goshen Road, R3 to M2): Motion to deny passed unanimously (5-0 - Curie, Dinwiddie, Perkins, Lewis, Sutton all voted deny)
Case ZC-2021-003 (TPT Holdings, 6600 Patricia Boulevard, R1/PBDD to M1): Discussion held but no final vote recorded in truncated transcript
action_items: Zoning director to notify Jeffrey Kelly of denial and schedule case before Board of Trustees
Zoning director to research original PBDD conditions and approved plans for the 6600 Patricia Boulevard property
Zoning director to investigate whether a PDM designation rather than straight M1 would be more appropriate for 6600 Patricia Boulevard
Mark Keeby (TPT Holdings) to explore renewed negotiations with Buzzard's Roost State Nature Preserve for alternative access from Gainer Road to bypass Patricia Boulevard neighborhood
key_amounts: $250,000 approximate investment by Jeffrey Kelly in truck repair building and business
17.736 acres - size of TPT Holdings property (ZC-2021-003)
34,500 square feet - existing building at 6600 Patricia Boulevard
5,000 square feet - TPT's current facility space on Route 28
40 tons of gravel placed by TPT Holdings on access road
25 platform tennis courts built per year on average
notable_topics: Zone change request from R3 to M2 for truck repair business at 6823 Goshen Road (denied)
Zone change request from R1/PBDD to M1 for platform tennis court assembly at 6600 Patricia Boulevard
Spot zoning concerns and precedent-setting implications
Disputed permits and prior zoning director's actions regarding Kelly property
Traffic, noise, and safety concerns on Patricia Boulevard from residents
Property value impacts on residential neighborhoods
Environmental concerns regarding oil and fluid runoff to O'Banion Creek
Easement and sewer access disputes between Stacey Briggs and TPT Holdings
Alternative access route through Gainer Road and state nature preserve
PBDD vs PDM vs straight M1 zoning classification discussion
County Planning Commission recommended denial for ZC-2021-002 and approval for ZC-2021-003
attendees: Mary Tom Graves Maffet (Resident, 6853 Goshen Road)
Mark Keeby (Owner, Total Platform Tennis / TPT Holdings)
notes: The transcript is truncated and the second case (ZC-2021-003) does not have a recorded final vote. Significant confusion exists around historical permitting records, particularly regarding a former zoning director's actions on the Kelly property and the original PBDD approval for the Patricia Boulevard property. Multiple residents expressed strong opposition to both zone changes. The Clermont County Planning Commission made opposite recommendations for the two cases: denial for ZC-2021-002 and approval for ZC-2021-003. The zoning director's presentation was notably thorough, identifying multiple code deficiencies for both properties.
title: ZC 8.3.2021
